title: Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 5000 Posts
shortInfo: Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Get the latest BOB recruitment notification 2026 for 5000 Apprentice vacancies under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The online application process starts on 19th May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 8th June 2026. Find Bank of Baroda Apprentice vacancies across all States/UTs on the official website http://bankofbaroda.bank.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of "Upcoming Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 apply

basic_details.job_title: Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 5000 Posts
basic_details.department: Human Resource Management (HRM), Bank of Baroda
basic_details.short_information: Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Get the latest BOB recruitment notification 2026 for 5000 Apprentice vacancies under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The online application process starts on 19th May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 8th June 2026. Find Bank of Baroda Apprentice vacancies across all States/UTs on the official website http://bankofbaroda.bank.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of "Upcoming Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 apply

important_dates.online examination date: To be notified on Bank's website and https://bfsissc.com
important_dates.document verification / language profici: To be notified after Online Examination result

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Application/Intimation/Examination Charges/Fees to BFSI SSC through the secure payment link provided in the application form. The fee must be paid through ONLINE mode only. No other mode of payment will be accepted. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years of age as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for reserved categories, subject to the condition that NAPS-registered candidates should be below 34 years as on 01-05-2026.

qualification.education_qualification: Graduation Degree in any Discipline from a recognized University or any equivalent qualifications recognized by the Central Government. For NATS candidates: date of passing Graduation should not be more than 4 years preceding 01-05-2026.
qualification.experience: Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after completing educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Candidates must be registered on Government Apprenticeship Portals (NATS and/or NAPS) before applying. Only Indian citizens are eligible to apply.

vacancy_details.State/UT: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

vacancy_details.Description: Locomotor Disability (OA, OL, BL, OAL), Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims and Muscular Dystrophy
Autism Spectrum Disorder, Specific Learning Disability and Mental Illness; & Multiple Disability
Disability amongst clauses above including deaf-blindness in the posts identified for each disability

category_wise_vacancy.Description: Disability amongst clauses above including deaf-blindness in the posts identified for each disability

selection_process: S No Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 60 Minutes Hindi / English
Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on three stages - Online Examination

and Test of local language of the State. Candidates must qualify all three stages. Final selection is subject to medical fitness as per the Bank's requirements. A State-wise and Category-wise Wait List will be published for vacancies arising from non-acceptance or non-reporting by selected candidates; this wait list remains valid for 1 year from result declaration or until the next engagement exercise

There will be NO negative marks for wrong answers. The online examination will be remotely proctored - candidates can appear from any location of their choice using a smart mobile phone / laptop / desktop / tablet with an operational front camera

and stable Wi-Fi internet connection. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order of aggregate marks. Tie-break: higher age is preferred.
Candidates qualifying the online examination will be called for Document Verification at a Bank-designated centre of the State/UT applied for
at their own expense. Bank of Baroda will not reimburse travelling expenses for Document Verification.

Candidates who produce 10th or 12th standard mark sheet having studied the specified local language of the State/UT will not be required to undergo the language test. Candidates who fail to qualify the local language test will not be eligible for engagement as apprentice.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register themselves on the Government Apprenticeship portals - NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in).

After completing registration on NAPS/NATS, eligible candidates will receive an email from [email protected] inviting them to fill the final "Application cum Examination Form" and make payment of the applicable fee/charges.

The application registration is considered complete only when fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC. Candidates must note down their Enrolment Id (NATS portal) and Apprentice Registration Code (NAPS portal) for all future correspondence.
Documents Required for Registration on NATS/NAPS Portals:
Colour scan copy of Aadhaar Card – Front & Back Page (JPEG, <1 MB)
Colour scan copy of PAN Card (JPEG, <1 MB)
Valid Personal Email ID and Mobile Number
Colour scan copy of Passport-size Photograph (JPEG, <1 MB)
Colour scan copy of Xth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
Colour scan copy of XIIth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
Colour scan copy of Degree Marksheet / Provisional Certificate (PDF, <1 MB)
Colour scan copy of Saving Bank Passbook / Cheque Leaf (PDF, <1 MB)
Colour scan copy of Signature (JPEG, <1 MB)
Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test:
Printout of valid Language Proficiency Test Intimation Letter/mail from Bank
Valid system generated printout of the online application / Registration / Enrollment form
Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate from Competent Municipal Authority or SSLC / Std. X Certificate with DOB)
Photo Identity Proof (Government-issued ID)
Individual Semester/Year-wise Marksheets and Certificates for educational qualifications including final degree/diploma certificate
CGPA to Percentage conversion certificate (if grades awarded), issued by the appropriate authority
Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates) - OBC-NCL certificate must be issued within one year as on date of advertisement
Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

Documents to be produced at the venue of Document Verification / Language Test. Non-submission of requisite documents will debar candidature from further participation in the selection process. Candidates must appear at their own expense.
Apply Online at: https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/ and https://bfsissc.com
important_instructions: This is NOT regular employment or contract employment in the Bank. Apprentices will be TRAINEES only and not employees of Bank of Baroda. The apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training. Bank of Baroda has no obligation to offer regular employment to apprentices during or after the apprenticeship period. Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only. Allotment to a different State/UT will not be considered under any circumstances. Candidates must first register on NATS and/or NAPS portals before applying; application without portal registration is not valid. The process of registration is complete only when applicable fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC. No Ex-servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an apprentice; there is no reservation for Ex-servicemen in this engagement. OBC candidates belonging to the 'Creamy Layer' are not entitled to OBC reservation and age relaxation; they should apply as General / General (OC/HI/VI/ID) as applicable. Change of category once submitted in the online application will not be permitted at any stage. Candidates should submit only one application; in case of multiple submissions, only the last valid completed application will be considered and fee paid for earlier registrations will be forfeited. The Apprentices are prohibited from joining / forming any Union Body. All correspondence will be made only on the registered email ID; candidates should keep it active throughout the recruitment process. No hard copy intimation will be sent by post for Online Examination. Candidates must appear for the selection process (Document Verification, Language Test) at their own cost; Bank will not reimburse travelling expenses. Apprentices will not be provided with Transport / Hostel facility or any other residential accommodation during training. Stipend of Rs. 15,000/- per month will be paid on completion of each month after adjusting Loss of Pay, if any. No other allowances/benefits are payable. All amendments/corrigendum (if any) will be published only on the Bank's website (https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/) and https://bfsissc.com - candidates should visit regularly. Any canvassing directly or indirectly shall disqualify the candidature. Use of unfair means, impersonation, or misbehavior will lead to disqualification and/or debarment. Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.

faq.question: Q1. What is Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is a nationwide engagement drive for 5000 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 for a training period of 12 months in various branches and offices of the Bank across 35 States/UTs. Apprentices will receive a stipend of Rs. 15,000 per month and will undergo On-the-Job Training but will not be treated as Bank employees and will have no claim to regular employment after completion of training.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 and how are they distributed?
faq.answer: A total of 5000 Apprentice training seats have been announced under Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 across 35 States/UTs, with State-wise and category-wise bifurcation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, UR and horizontal reservation for PwBD candidates. For example, major States include Gujarat – 960 seats, Uttar Pradesh – 764 seats, Maharashtra – 580 seats, Rajasthan – 412 seats, Karnataka – 483 seats and Tamil Nadu – 199 seats, while smaller States/UTs such as Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Manipur and Sikkim have 1 seat each.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: As per Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01 dated 19/05/2026, the online application process for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 starts from 19 May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 8 June 2026. The cut‑off date for age and educational qualification eligibility is 1 May 2026, while the Online Examination date and schedule for Document Verification and Local Language Test will be notified later on the Bank’s career page and BFSI SSC’s website.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria for Bank of Baroda Apprentice 2026 (education, age and nationality)?
faq.answer: Candidates must be Indian citizens, possess a Graduation Degree in any discipline from a recognized University (or equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government), and for NATS‑registered candidates the date of passing graduation must not be more than 4 years prior to 01‑05‑2026. The age limit is 20 to 28 years as on 01‑05‑2026, with upper age relaxation for SC/ST, OBC (NCL) and PwBD as per Government norms, subject to the condition that NAPS‑registered candidates must be below 34 years on the cut‑off date.

faq.question: Q5. Is prior apprenticeship or work experience allowed for BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates who have already undergone apprenticeship training with Bank of Baroda or any other establishment in the past, or who are currently undergoing apprenticeship training with any organization, are not eligible to apply. Further, applicants who have one year or more of training or job experience after completing their educational qualification are also not eligible for engagement as apprentices under this recruitment.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process for BOB Apprentice 2026 consists of three stages – Online Examination, Document Verification and Test of Local Language of the State/UT applied for, and candidates must qualify each stage to be finally engaged as apprentices. The online exam carries 100 questions for 100 marks across four sections (General/Financial Awareness, Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude, Computer Knowledge and General English), has no negative marking, and a State‑wise and category‑wise merit list will be prepared based on aggregate marks with tie‑breaking in favour of higher age.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any negative marking in the Bank of Baroda Apprentice 2026 online exam and what is the exam pattern?
faq.answer: There is no negative marking for wrong answers in the Bank of Baroda Apprentice online examination, which is an important advantage for candidates attempting maximum questions. The exam consists of 4 tests – General/Financial Awareness, Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude, Computer Knowledge and General English – with 25 questions and 25 marks for each section, totaling 100 questions and 100 marks, and is conducted in bilingual mode (Hindi/English) except General English which is in English only.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 and how can it be paid?
faq.answer: For Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026, General/EWS/OBC male candidates must pay Rs. 800 plus GST, General/EWS/OBC female and transgender candidates must pay Rs. 600 plus GST, while SC/ST and PwBD candidates are required to pay Rs. 200 plus GST as non‑refundable application/intimation/examination charges. The fee can be paid only in online mode through the secure payment link of BFSI Sector Skill Council of India (BFSI SSC) integrated in the application process, and applications without successful fee payment will not be treated as complete.

faq.question: Q9. How to apply online for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 and what registrations are mandatory?
faq.answer: Candidates must first complete mandatory registration on Government apprenticeship portals – NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) – and only then proceed for Bank of Baroda Apprentice application. After NATS/NAPS registration, eligible candidates will receive an email from BFSI SSC ([email protected]) containing the link for “Application‑cum‑Examination Form”; candidates must fill this form with their NATS Enrolment ID and/or NAPS Apprentice Code, upload required documents, choose State/UT and preferred locations, and finally pay the applicable fee online to complete registration.

faq.question: Q10. Does Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 provide permanent bank jobs after training?
faq.answer: No, the Bank of Baroda Apprentice engagement is purely a training arrangement under the Apprentices Act, 1961 and does not constitute regular or contractual employment in the Bank. Apprentices will not have any right or claim for absorption or regular appointment in Bank of Baroda after completion of the 12‑month apprenticeship period, and the Bank is under no obligation to offer any job during or after the training.

faq.answer: As per Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01 dated 19/05/2026, the online application process for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 starts from 19 May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 8 June 2026. faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria for Bank of Baroda Apprentice 2026 (education, age and nationality)?
faq.answer: Candidates must be Indian citizens, possess a Graduation Degree in any discipline from a recognized University (or equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government), and for NATS‑registered candidates the date of passing graduation must not be more than 4 years prior to 01‑05‑2026. The age limit is 20 to 28 years as on 01‑05‑2026, with upper age relaxation for SC/ST, OBC (NCL) and PwBD as per Government norms, subject to the condition that NAPS‑registered candidates must be below 34 years on the cut‑off date.

faq.question: Q5. Is prior apprenticeship or work experience allowed for BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates who have already undergone apprenticeship training with Bank of Baroda or any other establishment in the past, or who are currently undergoing apprenticeship training with any organization, are not eligible to apply. Further, applicants who have one year or more of training or job experience after completing their educational qualification are also not eligible for engagement as apprentices under this recruitment.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process for BOB Apprentice 2026 consists of three stages – Online Examination, Document Verification and Test of Local Language of the State/UT applied for, and candidates must qualify each stage to be finally engaged as apprentices. The online exam carries 100 questions for 100 marks across four sections (General/Financial Awareness, Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude, Computer Knowledge and General English), has no negative marking, and a State‑wise and category‑wise merit list will be prepared based on aggregate marks with tie‑breaking in favour of higher age.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any negative marking in the Bank of Baroda Apprentice 2026 online exam and what is the exam pattern?
faq.answer: There is no negative marking for wrong answers in the Bank of Baroda Apprentice online examination, which is an important advantage for candidates attempting maximum questions. The exam consists of 4 tests – General/Financial Awareness, Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude, Computer Knowledge and General English – with 25 questions and 25 marks for each section, totaling 100 questions and 100 marks, and is conducted in bilingual mode (Hindi/English) except General English which is in English only.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 and how can it be paid?
faq.answer: For Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026, General/EWS/OBC male candidates must pay Rs. 800 plus GST, General/EWS/OBC female and transgender candidates must pay Rs. 600 plus GST, while SC/ST and PwBD candidates are required to pay Rs. 200 plus GST as non‑refundable application/intimation/examination charges. The fee can be paid only in online mode through the secure payment link of BFSI Sector Skill Council of India (BFSI SSC) integrated in the application process, and applications without successful fee payment will not be treated as complete.

faq.question: Q9. How to apply online for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 and what registrations are mandatory?
faq.answer: Candidates must first complete mandatory registration on Government apprenticeship portals – NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) – and only then proceed for Bank of Baroda Apprentice application. After NATS/NAPS registration, eligible candidates will receive an email from BFSI SSC ([email protected]) containing the link for “Application‑cum‑Examination Form”; candidates must fill this form with their NATS Enrolment ID and/or NAPS Apprentice Code, upload required documents, choose State/UT and preferred locations, and finally pay the applicable fee online to complete registration.

faq.question: Q10. Does Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 provide permanent bank jobs after training?
faq.answer: No, the Bank of Baroda Apprentice engagement is purely a training arrangement under the Apprentices Act, 1961 and does not constitute regular or contractual employment in the Bank. Apprentices will not have any right or claim for absorption or regular appointment in Bank of Baroda after completion of the 12‑month apprenticeship period, and the Bank is under no obligation to offer any job during or after the training.

faq.question: What is Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is a nationwide engagement drive for 5000 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 for a training period of 12 months in various branches and offices of the Bank across 35 States/UTs. Apprentices will receive a stipend of Rs. 15,000 per month and will undergo On-the-Job Training but will not be treated as Bank employees and will have no claim to regular employment after completion of training.

faq.question: How many vacancies are there in BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 and how are they distributed?
faq.answer: A total of 5000 Apprentice training seats have been announced under Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 across 35 States/UTs, with State-wise and category-wise bifurcation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, UR and horizontal reservation for PwBD candidates. For example, major States include Gujarat – 960 seats, Uttar Pradesh – 764 seats, Maharashtra – 580 seats, Rajasthan – 412 seats, Karnataka – 483 seats and Tamil Nadu – 199 seats, while smaller States/UTs such as Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Manipur and Sikkim have 1 seat each.
